title: Data Handling: Finding the Average
subject: Math
intro_hook: When Virat Kohli's 'average' is 50, does it mean he scores exactly 50 in every match? How is it calculated?
big_idea: The mean (average) is a way to find a single 'typical' number that represents a whole group of different numbers.

question_flow.q: If you have 10 chocolates, and I have 2, how can we share them so we both have the same amount?
What you just did is 'averaging'. How would you describe that process in math terms?
Why is an average useful when looking at cricket scores or class marks?

indian_analogies: Calculating the mileage of a bike (average km per litre over several days).

misconceptions.belief: The average must be one of the numbers in the data.
misconceptions.probe: If one person is 150cm tall and another is 160cm, the average is 155cm. Does anyone have to actually be 155cm?

whiteboard.title: Average (Mean)
whiteboard.goal: Find the typical value of a dataset.
basics: Average (Mean) = Leveling out the differences so everyone has an equal share.
It gives one number to represent many.
whiteboard.formula: Mean = (Sum of all values) / (Total number of values)

symbols.example: Σx means add them all up

steps.label: Gather Data
steps.detail: List all the numbers you are examining.

steps.label: Sum
steps.detail: Add all the numbers together to find the total.

steps.label: Count
steps.detail: Count how many numbers there are.

steps.label: Divide
steps.detail: Divide the total sum by the count.

whiteboard.feedback_prompt: Can you explain why we divide by the total count?

common_confusions.fix: The sum is the total, but the average is the PER PERSON share.
